title: Rest, consolidation, and preview for Catch-up, comprehensive review, and study-system reset
week_focus: Catch-up, comprehensive review, and study-system reset
checkpoint: Everything is either complete or deliberately parked with a next step.
resource_names: Your notes, workbook, and saved artifacts
plain_language_intro: Today is a light consolidation day for catch-up, comprehensive review, and study-system reset.
mini_lecture_markdown:
Today is a rest day for Catch-up, comprehensive review, and study-system reset.

Keep the week lightly in view, but do not turn the day into a full study session. Use the pause to reset before the next study block.

The main goal is to preserve continuity without adding another heavy block of material.

A short preview of the next week is enough to keep continuity without turning the day back into a long session.

The rest day exists to preserve momentum and clarity rather than add another dense topic.
exam_relevance_markdown: Rest days matter indirectly for Cross-certification review (A+ / Network+ / Security+) because spaced retrieval and reduced overload help you keep earlier concepts available when scenario questions combine multiple topics.
key_distinctions: Recognition vs diagnosis — knowing the term name is not enough; know what evidence would make you pick it in a scenario.
mental_model_markdown: Picture the week as a simple chain of concept, mechanism, evidence, and decision.
self_check_prompts: Walk through today's objective in sequence and say which step would fail first if the system were misconfigured.
Connect today's glossary terms to this week's deliverable: what part of the artifact proves that you understand the mechanism and not just the label?
What idea from this week would still confuse you if it appeared inside a short scenario tomorrow?
quick_recap: Today keeps catch-up, comprehensive review, and study-system reset organized without adding more heavy content.

resource_anchors.label: Your notes, workbook, and saved artifacts
